Description: Define MVP scope and finalize backend microservices architecture
Initialize repos, Docker Compose, CI/CD pipeline
JWT authentication, RBAC, user profile management
Wallet creation, balance, immutable ledger, history
Stripe checkout, webhooks, reconciliation
Security event collection, IP blocklist, SQLi detection, audit trail
Outbox pattern, idempotent consumers, dead-letter topics
Email notifications for payments, retry logic, delivery journal
Risk scoring (rules + ML + Ollama), policy actions, Kafka async
Payment/wallet/security reports, Grafana dashboards, Prometheus, Loki
Unit/integration/E2E tests, security hardening, CI/CD, production deploy

Summary: Create GitHub repo with branch strategy (main/dev/feature/*)
Description: Create mono-repo or multi-repo structure. Add branch protection rules on main. Convention: main/dev/feature/*

Summary: Add PR templates and review rules
Description: Create pull request templates, define code review requirements (min 1 approval)

Summary: Write docker-compose.yml (PostgreSQL + Redis + Kafka + Grafana + Loki)
Description: Docker Compose with containers: PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka + Zookeeper, Grafana, Loki. Include health checks.

Summary: Add health check endpoints for each service
Description: Each .NET microservice exposes GET /health returning service status

Summary: Write README with local setup instructions
Description: Document prerequisites (Docker, .NET 8, Node), steps to clone and run, API examples (curl/Postman)
User table: id, email, name, password_hash, google_id, mfa_enabled, account_status, created_at, updated_at, last_login_at. Run EF Core migration.
Account table: id, user_id, account_number, type, currency, balance, created_at, updated_at
Ledger table: id, account_id, type (credit/debit), amount, balance_after, description, created_at. Append-only.
LoginEvent table: id, user_id, ip, user_agent, country, success, failure_reason, timestamp, is_flagged
Register endpoint: validate input, hash password (bcrypt 12 rounds), create user, auto-create wallet (balance=0), return JWT + refresh token (httpOnly cookie)
Login endpoint: validate credentials, issue JWT (15 min) + refresh token (7 days), log LoginEvent (ip, user_agent, success/fail)
Refresh token endpoint: validate refresh token, issue new JWT access token
Logout endpoint: invalidate refresh token, clear httpOnly cookie
Attribute-based authorization on .NET controllers. Roles: User, Admin. Reject unauthorized with 403.
When register succeeds, create Account + Wallet with balance=0. Link to user_id.
Return current wallet balance for authenticated user. Query Account table by user_id.
Manual test: register -> login -> get balance -> refresh token -> logout. Verify Docker Compose starts cleanly.
Return authenticated user profile: name, email, created_at, preferences
Update name, language, notification preferences. Log sensitive changes to audit table.

Summary: Implement GET /api/wallet/history with pagination and filters
Description: Paginated ledger entries. Query params: startDate, endDate, type (credit/debit), page, size. Sort by timestamp desc.
Return detailed view of a single ledger entry by ID.
Email/password login form. Google Sign-In button. Route to dashboard on success.
Registration form: name, email, password, confirm password. Validation. Redirect to login on success.
Wallet balance card, recent transactions list (last 10), navigation to Profile/History/Settings
Display user profile fields. Edit form for name and preferences. Save calls PUT /api/users/profile.
Table with columns: date, type, amount, balance_after, description. Date/type filters. Pagination controls.

Summary: Angular: Auth guards and JWT HTTP interceptor
Description: Route guard: redirect to /login if no valid JWT. HTTP interceptor: attach Authorization header, handle 401 -> redirect to login.
Receive Google OAuth token, verify with Google API, create or link user, issue JWT + refresh token.
Add Google Sign-In button on login page. Handle OAuth redirect flow. Store JWT on success.

Summary: Unit tests: profile CRUD and wallet history
Description: Unit tests for GET/PUT profile, GET wallet history with filters. Mock DB layer.
Manual E2E: register -> login -> view dashboard -> edit profile -> view history -> Google OAuth login
Input: serviceType, amount. Create Stripe CheckoutSession. Save transaction with status=PENDING. Return Stripe session URL.
Verify Stripe signature. On completed: set SUCCESS, credit wallet via ledger. On expired: set FAILED. Write outbox event.

Summary: Create outbox_events table and Kafka publisher worker
Description: outbox_events table: id, topic, payload, status (PENDING/SENT/FAILED), created_at. Background worker polls PENDING, publishes to Kafka, marks SENT. Retry with backoff.
Check event_id before processing. Retry policy on failure. Dead-letter topic (DLT) for permanently failed messages.
Consume payment.completed and payment.failed topics. Send email using templates. Record notification status. Retry with exponential backoff (max 3).
HTML email templates: success (amount, date, tx ID), failure (reason, retry suggestion). Use SMTP for sending.
Generate idempotency key per payment request. Prevent duplicate Stripe sessions for same payment intent.
Scheduled job: compare local transactions vs Stripe. Flag mismatches for admin review.
Payment page: service selector, amount input, Pay button. Redirect to Stripe Checkout. Success/cancel return pages.

Summary: Angular: show payment transactions in dashboard + notification preferences
Description: Update dashboard to include payment transactions in wallet history. Add notification preferences toggle in profile settings.

Summary: Sprint 3 testing: checkout + webhook + notification flow
Description: Unit tests: checkout creation, webhook signature. Integration: session -> webhook -> ledger -> notification. Idempotency: double submit -> single charge.

Summary: Security Service: capture login events (ip, user_agent, country, correlation_id)
Description: Middleware captures ip, user_agent, GeoIP country on every login. Store in security_events table. Add correlation_id header propagation.
5 failed logins/min per IP -> temp block. 100 req/min per IP -> 429 response. Redis counters with TTL.
BlockList table + endpoints: GET /api/admin/blocklist, POST (add), DELETE (remove). Middleware checks IP against Redis-cached blocklist.
Pattern matching: '; DROP, UNION SELECT, OR 1=1, comment patterns. Log as AttackEvent. Auto-block IP on repeated attempts.

Summary: Security Service: Immutable audit trail (hash chain)
Description: audit_log table with prev_hash/curr_hash. curr_hash = SHA256(prev_hash + action + timestamp + data). GET /api/admin/security/timeline endpoint.

Summary: AI Risk Service: Kafka consumer for security.event.created
Description: Consume security events from Kafka. Route to scoring pipeline.

Summary: AI Risk Service: Rule-based risk scoring
Description: Score calculation: failed login +20, VPN +15, unusual hour +10, new device +15, high-risk country +15, impossible travel +40

Summary: AI Risk Service: ML anomaly detection (Isolation Forest)
Description: Train Isolation Forest on historical login events. Features: login_hour, country_code, device_hash, attempts_count. Output anomaly score.

Summary: AI Risk Service: Ollama integration (Mistral 7B threat analysis)
Description: Call Ollama (Mistral 7B) to analyze event context, generate human-readable threat description. Include in risk.score.computed event.

Summary: AI Risk Service: Publish risk.score.computed + action policies
Description: Publish to Kafka: score, recommendation. Policies: 0-20 ALLOW, 21-50 CHALLENGE, 51-80 TEMP_BLOCK, >80 BLOCK. Fallback to rules-only if AI down.

Summary: Security Service: consume risk scores and apply actions
Description: Consume risk.score.computed. Update blocklist, lock account, alert admin on critical (>80).
Attacks today count + trend. Attack timeline table (IP, type, risk score, action). Blocked IPs list with unblock button.
User list with search/filter. Lock/unlock account buttons. View user login events.
Current blocklist display. Add/remove IP forms. Show IP reputation (score, country, VPN).

Summary: Setup Prometheus metrics scraping for all .NET services
Description: Expose /metrics on each service. Prometheus config to scrape. Metrics: request_count, duration, errors, login success/fail, kafka messages.
Security: attacks/day, blocked IPs, failed logins, SQLi. Payments: tx/day, success rate. System: CPU, RAM, latency, errors.

Summary: Configure Loki log aggregation + Grafana alert rules
Description: Loki collects logs from all services. Alert rules in Grafana: notify admin email on critical thresholds.

Summary: Sprint 4 testing: security + AI scoring flow
Description: Unit tests: risk scoring, blocklist ops, hash chain. Integration: login -> event -> Kafka -> AI score -> block. Verify Grafana dashboards.
Payment aggregates: daily/weekly/monthly totals. Wallet KPIs: volume, active wallets, avg balance. Security summary: attacks by type, top IPs.
Auto-generated daily report endpoint returning payment, wallet, and security summaries.
Custom report generation: date range, report type (payments/security/wallet). Return aggregated data.

Summary: Angular: Admin reporting page with charts and export
Description: Date range picker, report type selector. Charts: daily transactions, attack trends, wallet growth. Export as CSV/PDF.

Summary: Unit tests for all critical services
Description: Auth (register/login/refresh/RBAC), Wallet (balance/ledger), Payment (checkout/webhook/idempotency), Security (blocklist/hash chain), AI Risk (scoring).
Stripe webhook flow, Kafka outbox flow, Security event -> AI score -> block flow. Mock external services.
User: register->login->pay->balance->history. Admin: login->attacks->block IP->reports. Load test: concurrent logins and payments.

Summary: Security hardening: CORS, headers, secrets, PII audit
Description: CORS strict whitelist. HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP headers. Secrets in env vars only. Verify no PII in logs. Input validation. SQL parameterization.
On push to dev: unit tests + lint + build Docker. On merge to main: full tests + build + push images + deploy + smoke tests. Secrets in GitHub Secrets.
K8s manifests: .NET services (2+ replicas), PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, Prometheus+Grafana+Loki. SSL/TLS. Health/readiness probes. DB backup schedule.
Generate Swagger from .NET controllers. Create architecture diagram (services, Kafka topics, data flow). Deployment guide. Admin user guide.
